gamma_events.title: Highest temperature in Munich on June 29?
gamma_events.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Munich Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 29 Jun '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Munich Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/de/munich/EDDM.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest model consensus from European and global ensembles points to a highest temperature in Munich on June 29 near 27–28 °C, with thundery showers and increased cloud cover expected to limit peak heating. This aligns with the market’s tight clustering around those outcomes, where traders weigh the risk of brief clear spells allowing 29 °C against more persistent instability that caps readings at 27 °C or below. Recent warm advection from the south has raised baselines above the June climatological average of roughly 22–24 °C, but an incoming Atlantic trough introduces timing uncertainty in the exact arrival of precipitation and its cooling effect. Official guidance from agencies such as the German Weather Service (DWD) and ECMWF runs will refine the maximum through the next 48 hours.

eventMetadata.context_description: Current forecast models indicate a moderating trend for Munich on June 30, with maximum temperatures likely near 25–27°C amid increasing cloud cover and patchy rain associated with an approaching frontal system. This follows several days of above-average warmth, with recent highs reaching 33–35°C, which has kept market-implied odds clustered around 28°C (32%) and 29°C (24%). Key variables include the exact timing of cooling advection, boundary-layer mixing, and potential for localized clearing that could allow brief spikes toward 29–30°C. Ensemble spreads remain wide this close to the event, with official guidance from agencies like the German Weather Service expected to refine the diurnal peak and precipitation timing over the next 48 hours.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Met Office guidance indicates London’s maximum temperature on June 30 will likely peak near 24 °C as the intense late-June heatwave relaxes. High pressure that drove mid-30 °C readings earlier in the week is giving way to fresher Atlantic air and scattered showers, moderating daytime maxima toward seasonal norms of 20–22 °C while still allowing modest warming under residual sunshine. Ensemble model consensus and the latest operational runs cluster tightly around 23–25 °C, with only limited upside risk if clearing skies persist longer than expected. This scientific outlook underpins the market’s leading 24 °C (38 %) and 25 °C (21.5 %) contracts, reflecting traders’ assessment of rapidly evolving synoptic conditions two days before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Forecast models from the National Weather Service and private sources indicate a high in the low 80s °F for New York City on June 28, driven by moderate southerly flow under a building high-pressure ridge with limited moisture and partial cloud cover limiting daytime heating. Recent observations show temperatures near seasonal normals for late June, with no strong heat advection or clear-sky amplification pushing values higher, while light winds and urban heat island effects support the 80–83 °F range that dominates market-implied odds. Ensemble guidance shows tight clustering around these thresholds, with only modest upside risk from any unexpected clearing or downslope warming. Traders appear to weigh this consensus heavily, as the probability of exceeding 85 °F remains low absent a rapid pattern shift before the daily maximum is recorded.

gamma_events.title: Highest temperature in NYC on June 29?
gamma_events.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the LaGuardia Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 29 Jun '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the LaGuardia Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ny/new-york-city/KLGA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Official National Weather Service forecasts for New York City point to mostly sunny skies and light winds on June 29, supporting surface heating under a building high-pressure ridge with minimal cloud cover or frontal activity. This setup favors a daily maximum in the mid-to-upper 80s, where small differences in afternoon insolation, boundary-layer mixing, or dewpoint advection can determine whether readings peak near 85°F or reach 86–87°F. Ensemble guidance shows tight clustering around these values, reflecting low uncertainty from recent model runs but sensitivity to exact timing of any residual moisture or seabreeze effects along the coast. Historical June climatology for Central Park places average highs near 81°F, so current conditions represent a modest positive anomaly driven by the dominant synoptic pattern.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Japan Meteorological Agency forecasts indicate mostly cloudy skies over Tokyo on June 29, with scattered showers possible earlier in the period, which limits daytime heating and supports maximum temperatures near seasonal norms of 25–27°C. Above-average temperature trends for the month, driven by broader atmospheric patterns and long-term warming, keep 26°C and 27°C as the closely matched market leaders, while reduced solar radiation from persistent cloud cover caps upside potential above 28°C. Official guidance shows minimal model divergence for this short-range period, with historical late-June baselines reinforcing the narrow 25–27°C range amid the ongoing rainy season. Traders are monitoring any last forecast updates on cloud breaks or frontal timing ahead of market resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Current forecast models indicate a moderating trend for Munich on June 30, with maximum temperatures likely near 25–27°C amid increasing cloud cover and patchy rain associated with an approaching frontal system. eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Met Office guidance indicates London’s maximum temperature on June 30 will likely peak near 24 °C as the intense late-June heatwave relaxes. High pressure that drove mid-30 °C readings earlier in the week is giving way to fresher Atlantic air and scattered showers, moderating daytime maxima toward seasonal norms of 20–22 °C while still allowing modest warming under residual sunshine. Ensemble model consensus and the latest operational runs cluster tightly around 23–25 °C, with only limited upside risk if clearing skies persist longer than expected. This scientific outlook underpins the market’s leading 24 °C (38 %) and 25 °C (21.5 %) contracts, reflecting traders’ assessment of rapidly evolving synoptic conditions two days before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Forecast models from the National Weather Service and private sources indicate a high in the low 80s °F for New York City on June 28, driven by moderate southerly flow under a building high-pressure ridge with limited moisture and partial cloud cover limiting daytime heating. eventMetadata.context_description: Official National Weather Service forecasts for New York City point to mostly sunny skies and light winds on June 29, supporting surface heating under a building high-pressure ridge with minimal cloud cover or frontal activity. This setup favors a daily maximum in the mid-to-upper 80s, where small differences in afternoon insolation, boundary-layer mixing, or dewpoint advection can determine whether readings peak near 85°F or reach 86–87°F. Historical June climatology for Central Park places average highs near 81°F, so current conditions represent a modest positive anomaly driven by the dominant synoptic pattern.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Tokyo Haneda Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/jp/tokyo/RJTT.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Japan Meteorological Agency forecasts indicate mostly cloudy skies over Tokyo on June 29, with scattered showers possible earlier in the period, which limits daytime heating and supports maximum temperatures near seasonal norms of 25–27°C. Above-average temperature trends for the month, driven by broader atmospheric patterns and long-term warming, keep 26°C and 27°C as the closely matched market leaders, while reduced solar radiation from persistent cloud cover caps upside potential above 28°C. Official guidance shows minimal model divergence for this short-range period, with historical late-June baselines reinforcing the narrow 25–27°C range amid the ongoing rainy season. Traders are monitoring any last forecast updates on cloud breaks or frontal timing ahead of market resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest model consensus from European and global ensembles points to a highest temperature in Munich on June 29 near 27–28 °C, with thundery showers and increased cloud cover expected to limit peak heating. This aligns with the market’s tight clustering around those outcomes, where traders weigh the risk of brief clear spells allowing 29 °C against more persistent instability that caps readings at 27 °C or below. Official guidance from agencies such as the German Weather Service (DWD) and ECMWF runs will refine the maximum through the next 48 hours.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Munich Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/de/munich/EDDM.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Current forecast models indicate a moderating trend for Munich on June 30, with maximum temperatures likely near 25–27°C amid increasing cloud cover and patchy rain associated with an approaching frontal system. This follows several days of above-average warmth, with recent highs reaching 33–35°C, which has kept market-implied odds clustered around 28°C (32%) and 29°C (24%). Key variables include the exact timing of cooling advection, boundary-layer mixing, and potential for localized clearing that could allow brief spikes toward 29–30°C. Ensemble spreads remain wide this close to the event, with official guidance from agencies like the German Weather Service expected to refine the diurnal peak and precipitation timing over the next 48 hours.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following an intense June heatwave that delivered multiple UK temperature records above 35°C, official guidance from the Met Office signals a transition to cooler maritime air and scattered showers across southeast England by June 29. Increased cloud cover and precipitation are expected to limit solar heating and cap daytime maxima, with ensemble forecasts showing modest spread around 23–26°C. The closely matched market probabilities for 24°C and 25°C reflect this uncertainty in exact timing of frontal passage and local urban heat-island effects, while lower odds for 27°C or higher align with the anticipated moderation from peak heatwave conditions.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Met Office guidance indicates London’s maximum temperature on June 30 will likely peak near 24 °C as the intense late-June heatwave relaxes. High pressure that drove mid-30 °C readings earlier in the week is giving way to fresher Atlantic air and scattered showers, moderating daytime maxima toward seasonal norms of 20–22 °C while still allowing modest warming under residual sunshine. Ensemble model consensus and the latest operational runs cluster tightly around 23–25 °C, with only limited upside risk if clearing skies persist longer than expected. This scientific outlook underpins the market’s leading 24 °C (38 %) and 25 °C (21.5 %) contracts, reflecting traders’ assessment of rapidly evolving synoptic conditions two days before resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Tokyo Haneda Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/jp/tokyo/RJTT.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Japan Meteorological Agency forecasts indicate mostly cloudy skies over Tokyo on June 29, with scattered showers possible earlier in the period, which limits daytime heating and supports maximum temperatures near seasonal norms of 25–27°C. Above-average temperature trends for the month, driven by broader atmospheric patterns and long-term warming, keep 26°C and 27°C as the closely matched market leaders, while reduced solar radiation from persistent cloud cover caps upside potential above 28°C. Official guidance shows minimal model divergence for this short-range period, with historical late-June baselines reinforcing the narrow 25–27°C range amid the ongoing rainy season. Traders are monitoring any last forecast updates on cloud breaks or frontal timing ahead of market resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Japan Meteorological Agency forecasts for Tokyo on June 30 show cloudy skies with daytime highs likely in the mid-to-upper 20s Celsius, consistent with seasonal norms near 28°C yet tempered by lingering tsuyu moisture and variable cloud cover. Above-average summer temperatures expected nationwide support the market's tight clustering around 27–28°C, while model runs highlight sensitivity to exact precipitation timing and Pacific high positioning that could nudge maxima slightly higher or keep them suppressed. Historical late-June variability in urban heat islands further contributes to trader uncertainty between these adjacent thresholds.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Met Office guidance indicates London’s maximum temperature on June 30 will likely peak near 24 °C as the intense late-June heatwave relaxes. High pressure that drove mid-30 °C readings earlier in the week is giving way to fresher Atlantic air and scattered showers, moderating daytime maxima toward seasonal norms of 20–22 °C while still allowing modest warming under residual sunshine. Ensemble model consensus and the latest operational runs cluster tightly around 23–25 °C, with only limited upside risk if clearing skies persist longer than expected. This scientific outlook underpins the market’s leading 24 °C (38 %) and 25 °C (21.5 %) contracts, reflecting traders’ assessment of rapidly evolving synoptic conditions two days before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Forecast models from the National Weather Service and private sources indicate a high in the low 80s °F for New York City on June 28, driven by moderate southerly flow under a building high-pressure ridge with limited moisture and partial cloud cover limiting daytime heating. Recent observations show temperatures near seasonal normals for late June, with no strong heat advection or clear-sky amplification pushing values higher, while light winds and urban heat island effects support the 80–83 °F range that dominates market-implied odds. Ensemble guidance shows tight clustering around these thresholds, with only modest upside risk from any unexpected clearing or downslope warming. Traders appear to weigh this consensus heavily, as the probability of exceeding 85 °F remains low absent a rapid pattern shift before the daily maximum is recorded.

eventMetadata.context_description: Official National Weather Service forecasts for New York City point to mostly sunny skies and light winds on June 29, supporting surface heating under a building high-pressure ridge with minimal cloud cover or frontal activity. This setup favors a daily maximum in the mid-to-upper 80s, where small differences in afternoon insolation, boundary-layer mixing, or dewpoint advection can determine whether readings peak near 85°F or reach 86–87°F. Ensemble guidance shows tight clustering around these values, reflecting low uncertainty from recent model runs but sensitivity to exact timing of any residual moisture or seabreeze effects along the coast. Historical June climatology for Central Park places average highs near 81°F, so current conditions represent a modest positive anomaly driven by the dominant synoptic pattern.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Met Office guidance indicates London’s maximum temperature on June 30 will likely peak near 24 °C as the intense late-June heatwave relaxes. High pressure that drove mid-30 °C readings earlier in the week is giving way to fresher Atlantic air and scattered showers, moderating daytime maxima toward seasonal norms of 20–22 °C while still allowing modest warming under residual sunshine. Ensemble model consensus and the latest operational runs cluster tightly around 23–25 °C, with only limited upside risk if clearing skies persist longer than expected. This scientific outlook underpins the market’s leading 24 °C (38 %) and 25 °C (21.5 %) contracts, reflecting traders’ assessment of rapidly evolving synoptic conditions two days before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Official National Weather Service forecasts for New York City point to mostly sunny skies and light winds on June 29, supporting surface heating under a building high-pressure ridge with minimal cloud cover or frontal activity. This setup favors a daily maximum in the mid-to-upper 80s, where small differences in afternoon insolation, boundary-layer mixing, or dewpoint advection can determine whether readings peak near 85°F or reach 86–87°F. Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Current forecast models indicate a moderating trend for Munich on June 30, with maximum temperatures likely near 25–27°C amid increasing cloud cover and patchy rain associated with an approaching frontal system. This follows several days of above-average warmth, with recent highs reaching 33–35°C, which has kept market-implied odds clustered around 28°C (32%) and 29°C (24%). Key variables include the exact timing of cooling advection, boundary-layer mixing, and potential for localized clearing that could allow brief spikes toward 29–30°C. Ensemble spreads remain wide this close to the event, with official guidance from agencies like the German Weather Service expected to refine the diurnal peak and precipitation timing over the next 48 hours.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Met Office guidance indicates London’s maximum temperature on June 30 will likely peak near 24 °C as the intense late-June heatwave relaxes. High pressure that drove mid-30 °C readings earlier in the week is giving way to fresher Atlantic air and scattered showers, moderating daytime maxima toward seasonal norms of 20–22 °C while still allowing modest warming under residual sunshine. Ensemble model consensus and the latest operational runs cluster tightly around 23–25 °C, with only limited upside risk if clearing skies persist longer than expected. This scientific outlook underpins the market’s leading 24 °C (38 %) and 25 °C (21.5 %) contracts, reflecting traders’ assessment of rapidly evolving synoptic conditions two days before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Forecast models from the National Weather Service and private sources indicate a high in the low 80s °F for New York City on June 28, driven by moderate southerly flow under a building high-pressure ridge with limited moisture and partial cloud cover limiting daytime heating. Recent observations show temperatures near seasonal normals for late June, with no strong heat advection or clear-sky amplification pushing values higher, while light winds and urban heat island effects support the 80–83 °F range that dominates market-implied odds. Ensemble guidance shows tight clustering around these thresholds, with only modest upside risk from any unexpected clearing or downslope warming. Traders appear to weigh this consensus heavily, as the probability of exceeding 85 °F remains low absent a rapid pattern shift before the daily maximum is recorded.

eventMetadata.context_description: Official National Weather Service forecasts for New York City point to mostly sunny skies and light winds on June 29, supporting surface heating under a building high-pressure ridge with minimal cloud cover or frontal activity. This setup favors a daily maximum in the mid-to-upper 80s, where small differences in afternoon insolation, boundary-layer mixing, or dewpoint advection can determine whether readings peak near 85°F or reach 86–87°F. Ensemble guidance shows tight clustering around these values, reflecting low uncertainty from recent model runs but sensitivity to exact timing of any residual moisture or seabreeze effects along the coast. The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Tokyo Haneda Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/jp/tokyo/RJTT.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.
